title: wedelia ciliata
intro: Wedelia ciliata, also known as the hairy wedelia or Mexican daisy, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family that is native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Wedelia ciliata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support immune function. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
